summary: Crystal‑clear audience tape of a tight late‑80s show with standout Bird Song, Foolish Heart and The Other One; superb sound and firing band.
review: The recording is an audience (AUD) capture that many reviewers say sounds as clean as a board, with excellent instrument separation and just a few muffled spots.  Musically the concert is tight from start to finish – Bird Song, Foolish Heart, The Other One, Dew and Hell in a Bucket get special praise, and guest David Hidalgo adds a tasty flavor.  Overall it’s regarded as one of the best late‑80s Dead shows.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance and audio quality across >5 detailed reviews; recording is described as superb and the show as tightly played with multiple standout moments.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear, punchy sound with great instrument separation; occasional crowd chatter and a few muffled spots but overall comparable to a board or matrix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Even, tight set with a classic ’88 repertoire; the first set builds to a powerful Bird Song finale and the second set revolves around an expansive The Other One, keeping momentum high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for killing it all night; his birthday show is called a monster performance.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass shines through, prominent and bright in the mix; many note how well his low end comes across.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s rhythm guitar is described as “on point” and complementary to Jerry’s lead work.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard) is ever‑present and taking charge; guest guitarist David Hidalgo adds tasty licks on a few tunes.
